basics.name: Andrew Hope
basics.summary: I have won over $74,000 in scholarships, awards, and fellowships for my academic and research work in undergrad.

teaching.description: This award was established in honor of Walter Gray, a renowned Physicist and former University of Michigan Professor. This fellowship is intended to support undergraduates for research at Fermilab, and I am the recipient in its inaugural year.

A Summer Research Institute for undergraduate researchers on DESI this summer, intended to bring together student DESI Fellows together across institutions to share their work and research more broadly. This is the inaugural cohort of Fellows.
The scholarship provides annual support to a junior-standing student with proven academic ability in the Department of Physics.

teaching.course: Otho Lyle Tiffany and Mary Lois Tiffany Fellowship
teaching.description: This prize is for non-graduating physics students who show great promise and progress in their academic efforts. This award is given to students based on faculty nominations and the deliberation of the Undergraduate Awards Committee.
Scholarship recipients demonstrate academic success, school and community involvement, leadership skills, a strong work ethic and exhibit creative thinking.
